title: How to Build a Full Stack Web App using MERN Stack
short_description: Learn how to build a complete full stack web application using MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js with real-world structure.
full_content:
The MERN stack is one of the most popular technologies for building modern web applications. It consists of MongoDB (database), Express.js (backend framework), React.js (frontend library), and Node.js (runtime environment).

Step 1: Setup Backend
Install Node.js and create a project using npm init. Install Express and create a basic server.

Step 2: Connect MongoDB
Use Mongoose to connect your app to MongoDB. Define schemas and models.

Step 3: Create API Routes
Build REST APIs for CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete).

Step 4: Setup React Frontend
Use create-react-app or Vite. Build UI components and connect APIs using Axios.

Step 5: State Management
Manage application state using React hooks or Redux.

Step 6: Authentication
Implement JWT-based authentication for login and signup.

Step 7: Deployment
Deploy backend on Render/Node server and frontend on Vercel or Netlify.

Real-world example: Blog app, e-commerce platform, portfolio dashboard.

Why MERN?
- Full JavaScript stack
- High performance
- Scalable architecture

This stack is widely used in startups and production systems.

title: Understanding REST APIs with Real Life Examples
short_description: A beginner-friendly guide to understanding REST APIs and how they work in real applications.
full_content:
REST API stands for Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface. It allows communication between client and server.

Example:
When you use a food delivery app, your app requests data from a server using APIs.

HTTP Methods:
GET - Fetch data
POST - Create data
PUT - Update data
DELETE - Remove data

Step-by-step working:
1. Client sends request
2. Server processes request
3. Server sends response (JSON format)

Real-world example:
Login system, payment gateway, weather apps.

Tools used:
- Postman (API testing)
- Express.js (backend)

Why REST?
- Lightweight
- Scalable
- Easy to integrate

Understanding REST APIs is essential for any developer.

title: How Firebase Works and Why Developers Use It
short_description: Learn how Firebase simplifies backend development with real-time database, authentication, and hosting.
full_content:
Firebase is a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform by Google that helps developers build apps faster.

Core Features:
- Realtime Database
- Firestore
- Authentication
- Hosting
- Cloud Functions

Step 1: Create Firebase Project
Go to Firebase console and create a project.

Step 2: Add App
Connect your Android/Web app with Firebase.

Step 3: Authentication
Enable login using Email, Google, or Phone.

Step 4: Database
Store and sync data in real-time.

Step 5: Hosting
Deploy your web app easily.

Real-world use:
Chat apps, login systems, real-time dashboards.

Why Firebase?
- No backend setup needed
- Real-time sync
- Easy integration

Firebase is ideal for beginners and rapid development.

title: What is JWT Authentication and How It Works
short_description: Understand JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and how they are used for secure authentication in modern apps.
full_content:
JWT (JSON Web Token) is a secure way to transmit information between client and server.

Structure:
Header.Payload.Signature

How it works:
1. User logs in
2. Server generates token
3. Token is sent to client
4. Client stores token (localStorage)
5. Token is sent with every request

Advantages:
- Stateless authentication
- Secure
- Scalable

Real-world example:
Login systems, protected routes, APIs.

Implementation:
Use libraries like jsonwebtoken in Node.js.

JWT is widely used in modern web applications for authentication.

title: Top 10 Git Commands Every Developer Must Know
short_description: Master essential Git commands that every developer should use in daily development workflow.
full_content:
Git is a version control system used to track code changes.

Important commands:
1. git init - Initialize repository
2. git clone - Copy repo
3. git add - Stage changes
4. git commit - Save changes
5. git push - Upload code
6. git pull - Fetch updates
7. git branch - Create branch
8. git checkout - Switch branch
9. git merge - Merge branches
10. git status - Check status

Real-world use:
Team collaboration, version tracking, code backup.

Why Git?
- Distributed system
- Collaboration friendly
- Industry standard

Git is a must-have skill for every developer.
